def _safe_frontmatter(review_path: Path) -> dict[str, str]:
    try:
        data, _ = parse_review_frontmatter(review_path)
    except Exception:
        return {}
    return data


def _findings_block(review_body: str) -> str:
    if "## Findings" not in review_body:
        return ""
    tail = review_body.split("## Findings", 1)[1]
    if "\n## " in tail:
        tail = tail.split("\n## ", 1)[0]
    return tail.strip()


def _finding_id(review_path: Path, index: int, severity: str) -> str:
    return f"{review_path.stem}#{severity.lower()}-{index}"


def _finding_text_from_line(line: str) -> str:
    text = line.strip()
    text = re.sub(r"^[\-*\d.]+\s*", "", text)
    text = re.sub(r"^\|", "", text)
    text = re.sub(r"\|$", "", text)
    parts = [part.strip() for part in text.split("|") if part.strip()]
    if len(parts) >= 2 and parts[0].upper() in {"HIGH", "MEDIUM", "LOW"}:
        return parts[-1]
    match = re.search(r"\b(HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW)\b[:\-]?\s*(.*)$", text, re.I)
    if match:
        return match.group(2).strip() or text
    return text


def _findings_from_review(review_path: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    try:
        meta, body = parse_review_frontmatter(review_path)
    except Exception:
        return []
    block = _findings_block(body)
    if not block:
        return []
    findings: list[dict[str, str]] = []
    index = 0
    for raw in block.splitlines():
        line = raw.strip()
        if not line:
            continue
        severity_match = re.search(r"\b(HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW)\b", line, re.I)
        if not severity_match:
            continue
        severity = severity_match.group(1).upper()
        index += 1
        findings.append(
            {
                "severity": severity,
                "id": _finding_id(review_path, index, severity),
                "finding": _finding_text_from_line(line),
                "source": review_path.name,
                "resolution_note": str(meta.get("resolution_note", "")).strip(),
            }
        )
    return findings


def _summarize_review(review_path: Path) -> str:
    try:
        meta, _ = parse_review_frontmatter(review_path)
    except Exception:
        return f"- {review_path.name} [unreadable]"
    status = str(meta.get("resolution_status", "")).strip() or "unknown"
    note = str(meta.get("resolution_note", "")).strip()
    findings = _findings_from_review(review_path)
    lines = [f"- {review_path.name} [{status}]"]
    if note:
        lines.append(f"  - resolution: {note}")
    for finding in findings:
        lines.append(f"  - {finding['severity']} {finding['id']}: {finding['finding']}")
    if len(lines) == 1:
        lines.append("  - findings: none")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _latest_artifact_sha(stage_state: dict) -> str:
    history = stage_state.get("adversarial_sha_history", [])
    if isinstance(history, list) and history:
        last = history[-1]
        if isinstance(last, str):
            return last
    initial_sha = stage_state.get("initial_sha", "")
    return str(initial_sha or "")


def _current_artifact_sha(stage: str, artifact_path: Path) -> str:
    if not artifact_path.exists():
        return ""
    return normalized_artifact_hash(stage, artifact_path)


def _git_merge_base_sha() -> str:
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "-C", str(REPO_ROOT), "merge-base", JUDGE_BASE_REF, "HEAD"],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        check=False,
    )
    if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
        return result.stdout.strip()
    return _git_head_sha(REPO_ROOT) or ""


def _git_diff(slug: str, base_sha: str) -> str:
    if not base_sha:
        return ""
    paths = _artifact_paths(slug)
    cmd = [
        "git",
        "-C",
        str(REPO_ROOT),
        "diff",
        "--unified=3",
        base_sha,
        "HEAD",
        "--",
        str(paths["spec"]),
        str(paths["plan"]),
        str(paths["tasks"]),
    ]
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
    return result.stdout if result.returncode == 0 else result.stdout or result.stderr or ""


def _build_variables(
    slug: str,
    stage: str,
    lens: str,
    stage_state: dict,
    prompt_override_text: str | None,
    diff_text: str,
) -> tuple[str, str, dict[str, str], str]:
    artifact_paths = _artifact_paths(slug)
    current_artifact_path = _stage_artifact_path(slug, stage)
    current_sha = _current_artifact_sha(stage, current_artifact_path)
    review_files = _review_files_for_stage(slug, stage)
    review_meta = [(path, _safe_frontmatter(path)) for path in review_files]
    latest_sha = _latest_artifact_sha(stage_state)
    all_findings = []
    for path in review_files:
        all_findings.extend(_findings_from_review(path))

    if lens == "completeness":
        variables = {
            "high_findings_with_ids": "\n".join(
                f"- {finding['id']}: {finding['finding']} ({finding['source']})"
                for finding in all_findings
                if finding["severity"] == "HIGH"
            )
            or "- No HIGH findings found.",
            "spec": artifact_paths["spec"].read_text(encoding="utf-8") if artifact_paths["spec"].exists() else "",
            "plan": artifact_paths["plan"].read_text(encoding="utf-8") if artifact_paths["plan"].exists() else "",
            "tasks": artifact_paths["tasks"].read_text(encoding="utf-8") if artifact_paths["tasks"].exists() else "",
        }
    elif lens == "restatement":
        latest_findings = []
        prior_lines = []
        for path, meta in review_meta:
            findings = _findings_from_review(path)
            if str(meta.get("artifact_sha256", "")) == latest_sha:
                latest_findings.extend(findings)
            else:
                prior_lines.append(_summarize_review(path))
        variables = {
            "prior_findings_and_fixes": "\n".join(prior_lines) or "- No prior findings yet.",
            "latest_findings": "\n".join(
                f"- {finding['severity']} {finding['id']}: {finding['finding']} ({finding['source']})"
                for finding in latest_findings
            )
            or "- No latest findings found.",
        }
    elif lens == "implementation-risk":
        base_sha = ""
        history = stage_state.get("adversarial_sha_history", [])
        if isinstance(history, list) and len(history) >= 2 and isinstance(history[-2], str):
            base_sha = history[-2]
        else:
            base_sha = str(stage_state.get("initial_sha", "") or "")
        variables = {
            "spec": artifact_paths["spec"].read_text(encoding="utf-8") if artifact_paths["spec"].exists() else "",
            "plan": artifact_paths["plan"].read_text(encoding="utf-8") if artifact_paths["plan"].exists() else "",
            "tasks": artifact_paths["tasks"].read_text(encoding="utf-8") if artifact_paths["tasks"].exists() else "",
            "diff_since_last_round": diff_text or _git_diff(slug, base_sha),
        }
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown lens: {lens}")

    system_prompt, user_template = load_prompt(lens)
    if prompt_override_text is not None:
        user_template = prompt_override_text
    prompt = substitute(user_template, variables)
    return system_prompt, prompt, variables, current_sha


def _validate_verdict(verdict: dict) -> str | None:
    required = ["judge", "model", "verdict", "confidence", "reasoning", "evidence", "timestamp"]
    for key in required:
        if key not in verdict:
            return f"missing required field: {key}"
    if verdict["judge"] not in VALID_LENSES:
        return f"invalid judge: {verdict['judge']!r}"
    if not isinstance(verdict["model"], str) or not verdict["model"].strip():
        return "model must be a non-empty string"
    if verdict["verdict"] not in {"proceed", "proceed-with-annotation", "block"}:
        return f"invalid verdict: {verdict['verdict']!r}"
    if not isinstance(verdict["confidence"], int) or not 0 <= verdict["confidence"] <= 5:
        return "confidence must be an integer in the range 0..5"
    if not isinstance(verdict["reasoning"], str) or len(verdict["reasoning"].strip()) < 10:
        return "reasoning must be a string of at least 10 characters"
    if "unaddressed_high_finding_ids" in verdict:
        if not isinstance(verdict["unaddressed_high_finding_ids"], list):
            return "unaddressed_high_finding_ids must be a list"
        for item in verdict["unaddressed_high_finding_ids"]:
            if not isinstance(item, str) or not item.strip():
                return "unaddressed_high_finding_ids items must be non-empty strings"
    if not isinstance(verdict["evidence"], list):
        return "evidence must be a list"
    for item in verdict["evidence"]:
        if not isinstance(item, dict):
            return "evidence items must be objects"
        for key in ("artifact", "section", "quote"):
            if key not in item or not isinstance(item[key], str):
                return f"evidence item missing string field: {key}"
    if not isinstance(verdict["timestamp"], str) or not verdict["timestamp"].strip():
        return "timestamp must be a non-empty string"
    # Tolerate harmless JSON-Schema metadata fields that judges sometimes
    # echo back when the prompt includes the full schema (Claude in particular
    # has been observed emitting `$schema` and `$id` alongside valid verdicts).
    # These have no semantic meaning for the vote, so stripping them silently
    # is correct and avoids false-block schema_violation fallbacks.
    IGNORABLE_METADATA_FIELDS = {"$schema", "$id", "$comment", "title", "description"}
    for meta_field in IGNORABLE_METADATA_FIELDS & set(verdict):
        verdict.pop(meta_field, None)
    extra = set(verdict) - set(required) - {"unaddressed_high_finding_ids"}
    if extra:
        return f"unexpected fields in verdict: {sorted(extra)}"
    return None


def _strip_markdown_json_fences(raw: str) -> str:
    """Strip a leading ```json / ``` code fence + trailing ``` if present.

    Claude and some Codex versions wrap JSON tool-use output in markdown
    code fences by default. The first production run (migration against
    orchestrator-split + finding-2-graphql-tightening) hit 2/6 schema_violation
    fallbacks purely because of fence wrapping; the actual JSON content was
    valid and substantive.
    """
    if not raw:
        return raw
    text = raw.strip()
    if text.startswith("```"):

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '520,860p' /Users/chrislaw/valuerank/.claude/worktrees/friendly-aryabhata-9efbf7/docs/workflow/operations/codex-skills/feature-factory/scripts/factory_cmd_judge.py" in /Users/chrislaw/valuerank/.claude/worktrees/friendly-aryabhata-9efbf7
 succeeded in 0ms:
    if text.startswith("```"):
        newline = text.find("\n")
        if newline != -1:
            text = text[newline + 1 :]
        else:
            text = text[3:]
    if text.endswith("```"):
        text = text[:-3]
    return text.strip()


def _attempt_model_call(
    slug: str,
    stage: str,
    round_number: int,
    lens: str,
    model: str,
    system_prompt: str,
    user_prompt: str,
    timeout_seconds: int,
) -> tuple[dict | None, str, str]:
    command = list(JUDGE_COMMAND_BY_LENS[lens])
    stub_dir = os.environ.get("JUDGE_STUB_DIR")
    if stub_dir:
        stub_candidates = [
            Path(stub_dir) / model,
            Path(stub_dir) / lens,
            Path(stub_dir) / f"judge.{model}",
            Path(stub_dir) / f"judge.{lens}",
        ]
        for stub_path in stub_candidates:
            if stub_path.exists():
                command = [str(stub_path)]
                break
    try:
        if command[0] == "codex":
            def _call() -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
                try:
                    return subprocess.run(
                        command,
                        input=f"{system_prompt}\n\n{user_prompt}",
                        capture_output=True,
                        text=True,
                        check=False,
                        timeout=timeout_seconds,
                    )
                except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
                    return subprocess.CompletedProcess(
                        command,
                        124,
                        stdout=exc.stdout or "",
                        stderr=exc.stderr or "",
                    )
        else:
            def _call() -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
                try:
                    return subprocess.run(
                        [*command, "--system-prompt", system_prompt, user_prompt],
                        capture_output=True,
                        text=True,
                        check=False,
                        timeout=timeout_seconds,
                    )
                except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
                    return subprocess.CompletedProcess(
                        [*command, "--system-prompt", system_prompt, user_prompt],
                        124,
                        stdout=exc.stdout or "",
                        stderr=exc.stderr or "",
                    )
        result = record_ai_call(slug, stage, round_number, "judge_panel", model, _call)
    except Exception as exc:
        return None, "", str(exc)

    stdout = result.stdout or ""
    stderr = result.stderr or ""
    if result.returncode != 0:
        return None, stdout, stderr or f"non-zero exit status {result.returncode}"
    cleaned = _strip_markdown_json_fences(stdout)
    try:
        verdict = json.loads(cleaned)
    except Exception as exc:
        return None, stdout, str(exc)
    if not isinstance(verdict, dict):
        return None, stdout, "response was not a JSON object"
    validation_error = _validate_verdict(verdict)
    if validation_error:
        return None, stdout, validation_error
    return verdict, stdout, ""


def _parse_with_retry(
    slug: str,
    stage: str,
    round_number: int,
    lens: str,
    model: str,
    system_prompt: str,
    user_prompt: str,
    timeout_seconds: int,
) -> tuple[dict, str]:
    schema_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / "judge_schema.json"
    schema_text = schema_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    appendix = (
        "\n\nYour previous response did not match the required JSON schema.\n"
        "Please emit ONLY a single JSON object matching this schema:\n"
        f"{schema_text}\n"
        "Do not include any prose outside the JSON.\n"
    )

    verdict, stdout, error = _attempt_model_call(
        slug,
        stage,
        round_number,
        lens,
        model,
        system_prompt,
        user_prompt,
        timeout_seconds,
    )
    if verdict is not None:
        return verdict, stdout

    retry_prompt = user_prompt + appendix
    verdict, retry_stdout, retry_error = _attempt_model_call(
        slug,
        stage,
        round_number,
        lens,
        model,
        system_prompt,
        retry_prompt,
        timeout_seconds,
    )
    if verdict is not None:
        return verdict, retry_stdout

    fallback = {
        "judge": lens,
        "model": model,
        "verdict": "block",
        "confidence": 0,
        "reasoning": f"schema_violation: {retry_error or error}",
        "evidence": [],
        "timestamp": _now_iso8601_utc(),
    }
    return fallback, retry_stdout or stdout


def _frontmatter_text(metadata: dict[str, object]) -> str:
    lines = ["---"]
    for key, value in metadata.items():
        lines.append(f'{key}: "{_escape_frontmatter(value)}"')
    lines.append("---")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _resolution_status(verdict: dict) -> str:
    return "accepted" if verdict["verdict"] in {"proceed", "proceed-with-annotation"} else "open"


def _resolution_note(verdict: dict) -> str:
    note = " ".join(str(verdict.get("reasoning", "")).split())
    return note if len(note) <= 240 else note[:237] + "..."


def _write_review_outputs(
    slug: str,
    stage: str,
    lens: str,
    verdict: dict,
    raw_output: str,
    current_sha: str,
    git_head_sha: str,
    git_base_sha: str,
) -> tuple[Path, Path, Path]:
    output_dir = reviews_dir(slug)
    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    verdict_path = output_dir / f"judge.{lens}.verdict.json"
    raw_path = output_dir / f"judge.{lens}.raw.txt"
    review_path = output_dir / f"judge.{lens}.review.md"

    atomic_json_write(verdict_path, verdict)
    raw_path.write_text(raw_output, encoding="utf-8")

    evidence_lines = [
        f"- {item['artifact']} :: {item['section']} - {item['quote']}"
        for item in verdict.get("evidence", [])
    ] or ["- None."]

    body = "\n".join(
        [
            f"# Review: {stage} {lens}-judge",
            "",
            "## Findings",
            "",
            str(verdict.get("reasoning", "")).strip(),
            "",
            "## Residual Risks",
            "",
            "\n".join(evidence_lines),
            "",
            "## Verdict (structured)",
            "",
            "```json",
            json.dumps(verdict, indent=2, sort_keys=True),
            "```",
            "",
            "## Resolution",
            f"- status: {_resolution_status(verdict)}",
            f"- note: {_resolution_note(verdict)}",
        ]
    )

    frontmatter = _frontmatter_text(
        {
            "reviewer": verdict["model"],
            "lens": f"{lens}-judge",
            "stage": stage,
            "artifact_path": repo_relative_path(_stage_artifact_path(slug, stage), REPO_ROOT),
            "artifact_sha256": current_sha,
            "repo_root": ".",
            "git_head_sha": git_head_sha,
            "git_base_ref": JUDGE_BASE_REF,
            "git_base_sha": git_base_sha,
            "generation_method": "judge-panel",
            "resolution_status": _resolution_status(verdict),
            "resolution_note": _resolution_note(verdict),
            "raw_output_path": repo_relative_path(raw_path, REPO_ROOT),
            "narrowed_artifact_path": "",
            "narrowed_artifact_sha256": "",
            "coverage_status": "full",
            "coverage_note": "",
        }
    )
    review_path.write_text(frontmatter + "\n\n" + body + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
    return verdict_path, raw_path, review_path


def _dispatch_panel(
    slug: str,
    stage: str,
    prompt_override_text: str | None,
    override_reason: str | None,
    timeout_seconds: int = JUDGE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
) -> tuple[list[dict], str, str]:
    state = load_workflow_state(slug)
    stage_state = _ensure_stage_state(state, stage)
    current_artifact_path = _stage_artifact_path(slug, stage)
    current_sha = _current_artifact_sha(stage, current_artifact_path)
    git_head_sha = _git_head_sha(REPO_ROOT) or ""
    git_base_sha = _git_merge_base_sha()
    history = stage_state.get("adversarial_sha_history", [])
    if isinstance(history, list) and len(history) >= 2 and isinstance(history[-2], str):
        diff_base = history[-2]
    else:
        diff_base = str(stage_state.get("initial_sha", "") or "")
    diff_text = _git_diff(slug, diff_base)

    prepared_prompts: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {}
    judge_round = _stage_int(stage_state, "judge_rounds") + 1
    for lens in JUDGE_LENS_ORDER:
        system_prompt, user_prompt, _, _ = _build_variables(
            slug,
            stage,
            lens,
            stage_state,
            prompt_override_text,
            diff_text if lens == "implementation-risk" else "",
        )
        if override_reason:
            user_prompt = f"{user_prompt}\n\nPrompt override reason: {override_reason}"
        prepared_prompts[lens] = (system_prompt, user_prompt)

    verdicts: dict[str, dict] = {}
    raw_outputs: dict[str, str] = {}

    def _worker(lens: str) -> tuple[str, dict, str]:
        model = JUDGE_MODEL_BY_LENS[lens]
        system_prompt, user_prompt = prepared_prompts[lens]
        heartbeat_set_activity(f"judge {lens} running")
        verdict, raw_output = _parse_with_retry(
            slug,
            stage,
            judge_round,
            lens,
            model,
            system_prompt,
            user_prompt,
            timeout_seconds,
        )
        _write_review_outputs(
            slug,
            stage,
            lens,
            verdict,
            raw_output,
            current_sha,
            git_head_sha,
            git_base_sha,
        )
        return lens, verdict, raw_output

    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=3) as executor:
        futures = {executor.submit(_worker, lens): lens for lens in JUDGE_LENS_ORDER}
        for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures):
            lens, verdict, raw_output = future.result()
            verdicts[lens] = verdict
            raw_outputs[lens] = raw_output

    ordered_verdicts = [verdicts[lens] for lens in JUDGE_LENS_ORDER]
    combined_raw = "\n\n".join(raw_outputs.get(lens, "") for lens in JUDGE_LENS_ORDER)
    return ordered_verdicts, current_sha, combined_raw, git_head_sha


def _payload_for_state(
    stage: str,
    outcome: str,
    proceed_count: int,
    block_count: int,
    *,
    next_action: str,
    reason: str,
    blockers: list[str],
    judge_round: int,
) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "next": next_action,
        "reason": reason,
        "blockers": blockers,
        "outcome": outcome,
        "proceed_count": proceed_count,
        "block_count": block_count,
        "timestamp": _now_iso8601_utc(),
        "judge_round": judge_round,
    }


def _build_exhausted_payload(slug: str, stage: str, state: dict, stage_state: dict) -> dict[str, object]:
    judge_round = _stage_int(stage_state, "judge_rounds")
    judge_history = stage_state.get("judge_verdicts", [])

   492	    # Auto-add files mentioned in the spec/artifact as context so reviewers

   494	    if args.stage in ("spec", "plan") and not getattr(args, "no_auto_context", False):
   495	        auto_context = _extract_file_paths_from_artifact(artifact_path, REPO_ROOT)
   496	        for p in auto_context:
   497	            if len(context_paths) >= _AUTO_CONTEXT_MAX_FILES:
   498	                break
   499	            if p not in context_paths:
   500	                context_paths.append(p)
   501	
   502	    if args.stage in STAGE_ARTIFACT_HEADINGS and not artifact_has_meaningful_content(args.stage, artifact_path):
   503	        raise SystemExit(
   504	            f"{args.stage} checkpoint requires a non-stub artifact first: {artifact_path}"
   505	        )
   506	    if args.stage == "diff":
   507	        diff_budget = prior_diff_budget or diff_review_budget_state(args.slug)
   508	        if (
   509	            diff_budget
   510	            and diff_budget.get("codex_review_present")
   511	            and diff_budget.get("large_artifact")
   512	            and diff_budget.get("artifact_exists")
   513	            and diff_budget.get("artifact_changed_since_codex")
   514	            and not args.allow_large_diff_rerun
   515	        ):
   516	            raise SystemExit(
   517	                "Large diff rerun would regenerate the Codex review for a "
   518	                f"{len(artifact_path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')) if artifact_path.exists() else 0}-character artifact. "
   519	                "Batch more implementation fixes before rerunning the diff checkpoint, "
   520	                "or pass --allow-large-diff-rerun if this spend is intentional."
   521	            )
   522	    if args.stage == "diff" and not args.use_existing_artifact:
   523	        if not scope_manifest.exists():
   524	            raise SystemExit("Diff checkpoint requires a saved scope manifest or explicit --path values")
   525	        run(
   526	            [
   527	                sys.executable,
   528	                str(WRITE_DIFF),
   529	                "--repo",
   530	                str(REPO_ROOT),
   531	                "--output",
   532	                str(artifact_path),
   533	                "--path-manifest",
   534	                str(scope_manifest),
   535	                *sum((["--allow-dirty-path", path] for path in allow_dirty_paths), []),
   536	                *([] if not args.base_ref else ["--base-ref", args.base_ref]),
   537	            ]
   538	        )
   539	        diff_meta_path = artifact_path.with_suffix(artifact_path.suffix + ".json")
   540	        if diff_meta_path.exists():
   541	            diff_meta = json.loads(diff_meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   542	            args.base_ref = diff_meta.get("git_base_ref") or args.base_ref
   543	    elif not artifact_path.exists():
   544	        raise SystemExit(f"Artifact does not exist: {artifact_path}")
   545	
   546	    if args.stage == "diff":
   547	        diff_meta_path = artifact_path.with_suffix(artifact_path.suffix + ".json")
   548	        if diff_meta_path.exists():
   549	            diff_meta = json.loads(diff_meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   550	            args.base_ref = diff_meta.get("git_base_ref") or args.base_ref
   551	            if args.use_existing_artifact:
   552	                recorded_head = diff_meta.get("git_head_sha", "")
   553	                if recorded_head:
   554	                    current_head = _git_head_sha(REPO_ROOT)
   555	                    if current_head and current_head != recorded_head:
   556	                        raise SystemExit(
   557	                            f"Existing diff artifact is stale: recorded HEAD {recorded_head[:12]} "
   558	                            f"does not match current HEAD {current_head[:12]}. "
   559	                            "Drop --use-existing-artifact to regenerate the diff, "
   560	                            "or pass --base-ref if you intentionally want to review an older slice."
   561	                        )
   562	
   563	    if args.stage == "diff" and artifact_path.exists():
   564	        diff_text = artifact_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   565	        if len(diff_text) > HARD_DIFF_ARTIFACT_MAX_CHARS:
   566	            raise SystemExit(
   567	                f"Diff artifact exceeds hard cap ({len(diff_text)} > {HARD_DIFF_ARTIFACT_MAX_CHARS}). "
   568	                "Split the review scope into smaller workflow paths or use a smaller diff artifact."
   569	            )
   570	        if len(diff_text) >= LARGE_DIFF_RERUN_WARN_CHARS:
   571	            print(
   572	                "warning: large diff artifact detected; future diff reruns will regenerate "
   573	                "the Codex review unless the artifact stays unchanged. Batch follow-up fixes when possible.",
   574	                file=sys.stderr,
   575	            )
   576	
   577	    artifact_sha = normalized_artifact_hash(args.stage, artifact_path)
   578	    cap_hit_payload: dict[str, object] | None = None
   579	    with with_locked_state(args.slug) as state:
   580	        stage_state = _ensure_stage_state_blob(state, args.stage)
   581	        current_rounds = _stage_int(stage_state, "adversarial_rounds")
   582	        if current_rounds >= 3:
   583	            cap_hit_payload = {
   584	                "next": "judge_panel",
   585	                "reason": f"{args.stage} reached adversarial round cap",
   586	                "blockers": [f"{args.stage}.adversarial_rounds >= 3"],
   587	            }
   588	            state["last_action_result"] = cap_hit_payload
   589	        else:
   590	            next_rounds = current_rounds + 1
   591	            stage_state["adversarial_rounds"] = next_rounds
   592	            stage_state["adversarial_sha_history"] = list(stage_state.get("adversarial_sha_history", []))
   593	            stage_state["adversarial_sha_history"].append(artifact_sha)
   594	            if not str(stage_state.get("initial_sha", "")).strip():
   595	                stage_state["initial_sha"] = artifact_sha
   596	            state[f"{args.stage}_adversarial_rounds"] = next_rounds
   597	
   598	    if cap_hit_payload is not None:
   599	        if args.json:
   600	            print(json.dumps(cap_hit_payload))
   601	        else:
   602	            print("→ next: judge_panel")
   603	        return 2
   604	
   605	    reviews_arg = getattr(args, "required_reviews", None)
   606	    if reviews_arg is None:
   607	        small_task_set = False
   608	        if args.stage in ("tasks", "closeout"):
   609	            # For "tasks": count from the artifact (tasks.md is the artifact).
   610	            # For "closeout": count from tasks.md in the workflow dir (artifact is closeout.md).
   611	            tasks_file = artifact_path if args.stage == "tasks" else workflow_dir(args.slug) / "tasks.md"
   612	            if tasks_file.exists():
   613	                try:
   614	                    task_text = tasks_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   615	                    task_count = sum(
   616	                        1 for line in task_text.splitlines()
   617	                        if line.strip().startswith("- [ ]") or line.strip().startswith("- [x]") or line.strip().startswith("- [X]")
   618	                    )
   619	                    if task_count < SMALL_TASK_SET_THRESHOLD:
   620	                        small_task_set = True
   621	                        print(
   622	                            f"info: small task set ({task_count} tasks < {SMALL_TASK_SET_THRESHOLD} threshold) — "
   623	                            f"skipping Gemini reviews for {args.stage} stage, running Codex review only.",
   624	                            file=sys.stderr,
   625	                        )
   626	                except Exception:
   627	                    pass  # count failed — fall back to full reviews
   628	        reviews_arg = required_reviews(
   629	            args.stage,
   630	            policy["sensitive"],
   631	            policy["large_structural"],
   632	            policy["performance_sensitive"],
   633	            policy["extra_gemini_lenses"],
   634	            fast=fast,
   635	            small_task_set=small_task_set,
   636	        )
   637	
   638	    manifest = checkpoint_manifest(
   639	        args.slug,
   640	        args.stage,
   641	        artifact_path,
   642	        args.base_ref,
   643	        context_paths,
   644	        reviews_arg,
   645	        args.max_artifact_chars,
   646	        args.max_context_chars,
   647	        args.max_total_chars,
   648	        allow_dirty_paths,
   649	    )
   650	    manifest_path = checkpoint_manifest_path(args.slug, args.stage)
   651	    atomic_json_write(manifest_path, manifest)
   652	    if args.stage == "diff":
   653	        if allow_dirty_paths:
   654	            update_workflow_state(
   655	                args.slug,
   656	                lambda state: state.__setitem__(
   657	                    DIRTY_OVERRIDE_KEY,
   658	                    {
   659	                        "allowed_dirty_paths": allow_dirty_paths,
   660	                        "used": True,
   661	                        "updated_at": int(time.time()),
   662	                    },
   663	                ),
   664	            )
   665	        else:
   666	            # No --allow-dirty-path on this run — clear any stale override from a prior run.
   667	            update_workflow_state(
   668	                args.slug,
   669	                lambda state: state.pop(DIRTY_OVERRIDE_KEY, None),
   670	            )
   671	
   672	    with HeartbeatEmitter(args.slug, args.stage):
   673	        heartbeat_set_activity("awaiting reviewers")
   674	        for review_spec in reviews_arg or []:
   675	            reviewer = str(review_spec.get("reviewer", "")).strip()
   676	            lens = str(review_spec.get("lens", "")).strip()
   677	            if reviewer and lens:
   678	                heartbeat_set_activity(f"dispatching {reviewer}.{lens}")
   679	                heartbeat_set_activity(f"reviewer {reviewer}.{lens} running")
   680	        cmd = [
   681	            sys.executable,
   682	            str(REPAIR),
   683	            "--checkpoint-manifest",
   684	            str(manifest_path),
   685	            "--workspace-dir",
   686	            str(REPO_ROOT),
   687	            "--gemini-timeout-seconds",
   688	            str(args.gemini_timeout_seconds),
   689	            "--gemini-retries",
   690	            str(args.gemini_retries),
   691	        ]
   692	        try:
   693	            result = subprocess.run(
   694	                cmd,
   695	                text=True,
   696	                timeout=args.repair_timeout_seconds,
   697	            )
   698	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   699	            if not args.fallback:
   700	                _rollback_adversarial_round(args.slug, args.stage)
   701	                print(
   702	                    f"checkpoint blocked: repair exceeded {args.repair_timeout_seconds}s for "
   703	                    f"{args.stage} on {args.slug}",
   704	                    file=sys.stderr,
   705	                )
   706	                return 1
   707	            fallback_reason = f"repair exceeded {args.repair_timeout_seconds}s"
   708	        except Exception:
   709	            _rollback_adversarial_round(args.slug, args.stage)
   710	            raise
   711	        else:
   712	            heartbeat_set_activity("aggregating results")
   713	            if result.returncode == 0:
   714	                reverted = revert_protected_files()
   715	                if reverted:
   716	                    print(f"reverted protected files: {', '.join(reverted)}", file=sys.stderr)
   717	                _advance_checkpoint_progress(args.slug, args.stage, pending_head_sha)
   718	                post_state = load_workflow_state(args.slug)
   719	                payload = _checkpoint_next_action_payload(args.slug, post_state, args.stage)
   720	                _persist_last_action_result(args.slug, payload)
   721	                heartbeat_set_activity("all reviews complete")
   722	                if args.json:
   723	                    print(json.dumps(payload))
   724	                else:
   725	                    _emit_next_action(args.slug, f"{args.stage} checkpoint")
   726	                return 0
   727	            if not args.fallback:
   728	                _rollback_adversarial_round(args.slug, args.stage)
   729	                return result.returncode
   730	            fallback_reason = f"repair exited {result.returncode}"
   731	
   732	        heartbeat_set_activity("reviewer gemini.requirements running")
   733	        fallback_ok, fallback_detail = run_checkpoint_fallback(
   734	            manifest_path,
   735	            REPO_ROOT,
   736	            args.gemini_timeout_seconds,
   737	            args.gemini_retries,
   738	        )
   739	        if not fallback_ok:
   740	            _rollback_adversarial_round(args.slug, args.stage)
   741	            print(f"checkpoint blocked: fallback review path failed for {args.stage} on {args.slug}: {trim_detail(fallback_detail)}", file=sys.stderr)
   742	            return 1
   743	        reverted = revert_protected_files()
   744	        if reverted:
   745	            print(f"reverted protected files: {', '.join(reverted)}", file=sys.stderr)
   746	        record_checkpoint_fallback(args.slug, args.stage, fallback_reason)
   747	        _advance_checkpoint_progress(args.slug, args.stage, pending_head_sha)
   748	        heartbeat_set_activity("aggregating results")
   749	        print(f"warning: fallback checkpoint path used for {args.stage} on {args.slug}: {fallback_reason}", file=sys.stderr)
   750	        post_state = load_workflow_state(args.slug)
   751	        payload = _checkpoint_next_action_payload(args.slug, post_state, args.stage)
   752	        _persist_last_action_result(args.slug, payload)
   753	        heartbeat_set_activity("all reviews complete")
   754	        if args.json:
   755	            print(json.dumps(payload))
   756	        else:
   757	            _emit_next_action(args.slug, f"{args.stage} checkpoint")
   758	        return 0

    28	@mutates_state("discover")
    29	def command_discover(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    30	    ensure_sync()
    31	    clear = getattr(args, "clear", False)
    32	    force_complete = getattr(args, "force_complete", False)
    33	    if clear and any(
    34	        [
    35	            args.required,
    36	            args.count is not None,
    37	            args.question,
    38	            args.recommendation,
    39	            args.rationale,
    40	            args.assumption,
    41	            args.summary is not None,
    42	            args.complete,
    43	            force_complete,

    45	            getattr(args, "resolve", None) is not None,
    46	            getattr(args, "defer", None) is not None,
    47	            getattr(args, "non_goal", None) is not None,
    48	            getattr(args, "acceptance_criteria", None) is not None,
    49	            getattr(args, "answer", None) is not None,
    50	        ]
    51	    ):
    52	        raise SystemExit("discover --clear cannot be combined with other discovery updates")
    53	    if args.count is not None and args.count < 0:
    54	        raise SystemExit("discover requires --count to be zero or greater")
    55	    if any([args.question, args.recommendation, args.rationale]) and not all(
    56	        [args.question, args.recommendation, args.rationale]
    57	    ):
    58	        raise SystemExit("discover requires --question, --recommendation, and --rationale together")
    59	    if not clear and not any(

    61	            args.required,
    62	            args.count is not None,
    63	            args.question,
    64	            args.assumption,
    65	            args.summary is not None,
    66	            args.complete,

    69	            getattr(args, "resolve", None) is not None,
    70	            getattr(args, "defer", None) is not None,
    71	            getattr(args, "non_goal", None) is not None,
    72	            getattr(args, "acceptance_criteria", None) is not None,
    73	            getattr(args, "answer", None) is not None,
    74	        ]
    75	    ):
    76	        raise SystemExit("discover requires at least one update, or use --clear to reset discovery state")
    77	
    78	    def mutate(discovery: dict) -> None:
    79	        if clear:
    80	            preserved_required = bool(discovery.get("required"))
    81	            preserved_question_count = discovery.get("question_count", 0)
    82	            if not isinstance(preserved_question_count, int):
    83	                preserved_question_count = 0
    84	            preserved_asked_count = discovery.get("asked_count", 0)
    85	            if not isinstance(preserved_asked_count, int):
    86	                preserved_asked_count = 0
    87	            preserved_questions = discovery.get("questions", [])
    88	            if not isinstance(preserved_questions, list):
    89	                preserved_questions = []
    90	            preserved_assumptions = discovery.get("assumptions", [])
    91	            if not isinstance(preserved_assumptions, list):
    92	                preserved_assumptions = []
    93	            preserved_summary = discovery.get("summary", "")
    94	            if not isinstance(preserved_summary, str):
    95	                preserved_summary = ""
    96	            preserved_answers = discovery.get("answers", {})
    97	            if not isinstance(preserved_answers, dict):
    98	                preserved_answers = {}
    99	            preserved_non_goals = discovery.get("non_goals", [])
   100	            if not isinstance(preserved_non_goals, list):
   101	                preserved_non_goals = []
   102	            preserved_acceptance = discovery.get("acceptance_criteria", [])
   103	            if not isinstance(preserved_acceptance, list):
   104	                preserved_acceptance = []
   105	            discovery.clear()
   106	            discovery.update(default_discovery_state())
   107	            discovery["required"] = (
   108	                preserved_required
   109	                or bool(preserved_question_count)
   110	                or bool(preserved_questions)
   111	                or bool(preserved_assumptions)
   112	            )
   113	            discovery["question_count"] = preserved_question_count
   114	            discovery["asked_count"] = preserved_asked_count
   115	            discovery["questions"] = preserved_questions
   116	            discovery["assumptions"] = preserved_assumptions
   117	            discovery["summary"] = preserved_summary
   118	            discovery["answers"] = preserved_answers
   119	            discovery["non_goals"] = preserved_non_goals
   120	            discovery["acceptance_criteria"] = preserved_acceptance
   121	            discovery["unresolved"] = []
   122	            discovery["complete"] = False
   123	            discovery["updated_at"] = int(time.time())
   124	            return
   125	        if args.required:
   126	            discovery["required"] = True
   127	        if args.count is not None:
   128	            if discovery.get("asked_count", 0) > args.count:
   129	                raise SystemExit(
   130	                    "discover requires --count to stay at or above the number of already asked questions; "
   131	                    "use --clear to restart discovery"
   132	                )
   133	            discovery["question_count"] = args.count
   134	            discovery["required"] = discovery["required"] or args.count > 0
   135	        if args.question:
   136	            questions = list(discovery.get("questions", []))
   137	            questions.append(
   138	                {
   139	                    "question": args.question,
   140	                    "recommendation": args.recommendation,
   141	                    "rationale": args.rationale,
   142	                    "updated_at": int(time.time()),
   143	                }
   144	            )
   145	            discovery["questions"] = questions
   146	            discovery["asked_count"] = len(questions)
   147	            discovery["required"] = True
   148	            if discovery.get("question_count", 0) < len(questions):
   149	                discovery["question_count"] = len(questions)
   150	        if args.assumption:
   151	            assumptions = list(discovery.get("assumptions", []))
   152	            for assumption in args.assumption:
   153	                if assumption not in assumptions:
   154	                    assumptions.append(assumption)
   155	            discovery["assumptions"] = assumptions
   156	            discovery["required"] = discovery["required"] or bool(assumptions)
   157	        if args.summary is not None:
   158	            discovery["summary"] = args.summary
   159	            discovery["required"] = discovery["required"] or bool(args.summary.strip())
   160	        if getattr(args, "answer", None) is not None:
   161	            question_text, answer_text = args.answer
   162	            if not isinstance(discovery.get("answers"), dict):
   163	                discovery["answers"] = {}
   164	            discovery["answers"][question_text] = answer_text
   165	        if getattr(args, "unresolved", None) is not None:
   166	            item_text = args.unresolved
   167	            unresolved = discovery.setdefault("unresolved", [])
   168	            if not any(u["item"] == item_text for u in unresolved):
   169	                unresolved.append({"item": item_text, "deferred": False})
   170	        if getattr(args, "resolve", None) is not None:
   171	            resolve_text = args.resolve
   172	            discovery["unresolved"] = [
   173	                u for u in discovery.get("unresolved", []) if u["item"] != resolve_text
   174	            ]
   175	        if getattr(args, "defer", None) is not None:
   176	            defer_text = args.defer
   177	            for u in discovery.get("unresolved", []):
   178	                if u["item"] == defer_text:
   179	                    u["deferred"] = True
   180	                    break
   181	        if getattr(args, "non_goal", None) is not None:
   182	            ng = discovery.setdefault("non_goals", [])
   183	            if args.non_goal not in ng:
   184	                ng.append(args.non_goal)
   185	        if getattr(args, "acceptance_criteria", None) is not None:
   186	            ac = discovery.setdefault("acceptance_criteria", [])
   187	            if args.acceptance_criteria not in ac:
   188	                ac.append(args.acceptance_criteria)
   189	        blocking = blocking_unresolved_items(discovery)
   190	        if args.complete:
   191	            if blocking:
   192	                if discovery_blockers_are_malformed(discovery):
   193	                    raise SystemExit(
   194	                        "discover cannot mark discovery complete while discovery state is malformed; "
   195	                        "use discover --clear to reset malformed discovery state"
   196	                    )
   197	                raise SystemExit(
   198	                    "discover cannot mark discovery complete while unresolved items remain; "
   199	                    "resolve or defer each item first"
   200	                )
   201	            if not force_complete and int(discovery.get("asked_count", 0)) < int(discovery.get("question_count", 0)):
   202	                raise SystemExit(
   203	                    "discover cannot mark discovery complete before the planned questions are recorded; "
   204	                    "use --force-complete if you intentionally want to override the count"
   205	                )
   206	            discovery["complete"] = True
   207	        elif (
   208	            args.required
   209	            or args.count is not None
   210	            or args.question
   211	            or args.assumption
   212	            or args.summary is not None
   213	            or getattr(args, "answer", None) is not None
   214	            or getattr(args, "unresolved", None) is not None
   215	            or getattr(args, "resolve", None) is not None
   216	            or getattr(args, "defer", None) is not None
   217	            or getattr(args, "non_goal", None) is not None
   218	            or getattr(args, "acceptance_criteria", None) is not None
   219	        ):
   220	            discovery["complete"] = False

   335	
   336	
   337	def parse_review_frontmatter(path: Path) -> tuple[dict[str, str], str]:
   338	    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   339	    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
   340	        raise ValueError(f"{path} is missing frontmatter")
   341	    _, rest = text.split("---\n", 1)
   342	    fm_text, body = rest.split("\n---\n", 1)
   343	    data: dict[str, str] = {}
   344	    for line in fm_text.splitlines():
   345	        if not line.strip():
   346	            continue
   347	        key, value = line.split(":", 1)
   348	        data[key.strip()] = value.strip().strip('"')
   349	    return data, body
   350	
   351	
   352	def load_scope_manifest(slug: str) -> dict:
   353	    path = scope_manifest_path(slug)
   354	    if not path.exists():
   355	        return {"paths": [], "allowed_dirty_paths": []}
   356	    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   357	
   358	
   359	def save_scope_manifest(slug: str, paths: list[str]) -> Path:
   360	    safe_slug = validated_slug(slug)
   361	    normalized_paths = {normalized_repo_path(path, "scope path").rstrip("/") for path in paths if path.strip()}
   362	    manifest = {
   363	        "paths": sorted(normalized_paths),
   364	        "allowed_dirty_paths": sorted(
   365	            {
   366	                *normalized_paths,
   367	                f"docs/workflow/feature-runs/{safe_slug}",
   368	            }
   369	        ),
   370	    }
   371	    path = scope_manifest_path(slug)
   372	    atomic_json_write(path, manifest)
   373	    return path
   374	
   375	
   376	def load_workflow_state(slug: str) -> dict:
   377	    """Load workflow state and add missing schema defaults without writing."""
   378	    path = factory_state_path(slug)
   379	    if not path.exists():
   380	        return _default_workflow_state()
   381	    state = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   382	    defaults = _default_workflow_state()
   383	    state.setdefault("review_policy", defaults["review_policy"])
   384	    state.setdefault(BLOCKED_KEY, defaults[BLOCKED_KEY])
   385	    state.setdefault(DISCOVERY_KEY, default_discovery_state())
   386	    state.setdefault(DELIVERY_KEY, {})
   387	    state.setdefault(DIRTY_OVERRIDE_KEY, {})
   388	    state.setdefault(CHECKPOINT_FALLBACK_KEY, {})
   389	    state.setdefault(PARALLEL_ANALYSIS_KEY, default_parallel_analysis_state())
   390	    state.setdefault(INIT_HEAD_SHA_KEY, "")

     1	# System Prompt
     2	You are a review-loop auditor.
     3	
     4	Your only job: determine whether the findings from the LATEST
     5	adversarial round are genuinely new issues, or whether they are
     6	restatements of themes already raised and addressed in earlier
     7	rounds.
     8	
     9	Review loops can go forever if each round produces cosmetic
    10	variations of already-handled concerns. Your output decides
    11	whether the loop is producing signal or noise.
    12	
    13	A finding is a RESTATEMENT if:
    14	- Earlier rounds raised the same underlying concern, AND
    15	- The orchestrator made a substantive change in response, AND
    16	- The new finding does not point at a new failure mode
    17	
    18	A finding is NEW if:
    19	- No earlier round raised this concern, OR
    20	- Earlier rounds raised it but the mitigation is now itself flawed
    21	  in a way the new finding surfaces
    22	
    23	## Verdict rules
    24	
    25	**First-round case (no prior findings exist):** You cannot assess
    26	restatement because there is nothing to compare against. Default
    27	to PROCEED-WITH-ANNOTATION in this case — the orchestrator needs
    28	signal from other judges (`completeness`, `implementation-risk`)
    29	to decide blocking, not from you. Note the findings you observed
    30	so later rounds have baseline to compare against.
    31	
    32	**Diminishing-returns case (prior rounds exist, latest findings
    33	are materially less severe than prior rounds):** PROCEED. Severity
    34	is ranked HIGH > MEDIUM > LOW. If prior rounds had HIGH findings
    35	and the latest round has only MEDIUM/LOW, the loop is converging.
    36	
    37	**True-saturation case (70%+ of latest findings are literal
    38	restatements of prior-round findings):** PROCEED.
    39	
    40	**Real-progress case (latest round surfaces genuinely new and
    41	material failure modes that were not covered before):** BLOCK.
    42	
    43	**Important non-blocking pattern:** a round that introduces new
    44	findings while prior findings remain addressed is NOT automatic
    45	block. Block only when the new findings point at load-bearing
    46	correctness or safety issues, not at cosmetic or belt-and-suspenders
    47	concerns.
    48	
    49	Be concrete. Quote specific text from both the old and new rounds
    50	when calling something a restatement.
    51	
    52	# User Prompt Template
    53	Earlier rounds' findings (with orchestrator responses):
    54	{prior_findings_and_fixes}
    55	
    56	Latest round's findings:
    57	{latest_findings}
    58	
    59	For each latest finding:
    60	1. Classify as NEW or RESTATEMENT
    61	2. If RESTATEMENT, quote the earlier finding it echoes and the
    62	   orchestrator response that addressed it
    63	3. If NEW, state the specific failure mode that was not previously
    64	   covered
    65	
    66	Then emit verdict JSON. Proceed = loop is saturated, block = loop
    67	is still finding real issues.

   144	def checkpoint_manifest(
   145	    slug: str,
   146	    stage: str,
   147	    artifact_path: Path,
   148	    base_ref: str | None,
   149	    extra_context: list[str],
   150	    reviews: list[dict[str, str]],
   151	    max_artifact_chars: int | None,
   152	    max_context_chars: int | None,
   153	    max_total_chars: int | None,
   154	    allow_dirty_paths: list[str] | None = None,
   155	) -> dict:
   156	    manifest_reviews = []
   157	    for spec in reviews:
   158	        output = reviews_dir(slug) / f"{stage}.{spec['reviewer']}.{spec['lens']}.review.md"
   159	        manifest_reviews.append(
   160	            {
   161	                "reviewer": spec["reviewer"],
   162	                "lens": spec["lens"],
   163	                "stage": stage,
   164	                "path": repo_relative_path(output, REPO_ROOT),
   165	                "context_paths": [repo_relative_path(resolve_stored_path(path, REPO_ROOT), REPO_ROOT) for path in extra_context],
   166	                **({"model": spec["model"]} if spec.get("model") else {}),
   167	            }
   168	        )
   169	    return {
   170	        "feature_slug": slug,
   171	        "stage": stage,
   172	        "artifact_path": repo_relative_path(artifact_path, REPO_ROOT),
   173	        "git_base_ref": base_ref or "",
   174	        "allowed_dirty_paths": list(allow_dirty_paths or []),
   175	        "required_reviews": manifest_reviews,
   176	        "max_artifact_chars": max_artifact_chars,
   177	        "max_context_chars": max_context_chars,
   178	        "max_total_chars": max_total_chars,
   179	    }
   180	
   181	
   182	def repair_checkpoint_args(slug: str, stage: str, state: dict[str, object]) -> argparse.Namespace:
   183	    manifest = load_checkpoint_manifest(slug, stage) or {}
   184	    workflow_state = load_workflow_state(slug)
   185	    context_paths: list[str] = []
   186	    for review in manifest.get("required_reviews", []):
   187	        for context_path in review.get("context_paths", []):
   188	            if context_path not in context_paths:
   189	                context_paths.append(context_path)
   190	    allow_dirty_paths = list(dict.fromkeys(manifest.get("allowed_dirty_paths", [])))
   191	    base_ref = manifest.get("git_base_ref") or ""
   192	    use_existing_artifact = True
   193	    diff_budget: dict[str, object] = {}
   194	    if stage == "diff" and not base_ref:
   195	        meta_path = Path(state["artifact_path"]).with_suffix(Path(state["artifact_path"]).suffix + ".json")
   196	        meta_manifest, _ = read_json_file(meta_path)
   197	        if meta_manifest:
   198	            base_ref = meta_manifest.get("git_base_ref") or base_ref
   199	            if not allow_dirty_paths:
   200	                allow_dirty_paths = list(meta_manifest.get("allowed_dirty_paths", []))
   201	    if stage == "diff":
   202	        diff_budget = diff_review_budget_state(slug)
   203	        if diff_budget.get("head_mismatch"):
   204	            base_ref = str(diff_budget.get("suggested_base_ref", "")) or base_ref
   205	            use_existing_artifact = False
   206	    if stage == "diff" and not allow_dirty_paths:
   207	        dirty_override = workflow_state.get(DIRTY_OVERRIDE_KEY, {})
   208	        allow_dirty_paths = list(dict.fromkeys(dirty_override.get("allowed_dirty_paths", [])))
   209	    return argparse.Namespace(
   210	        slug=slug,
   211	        stage=stage,
   212	        artifact=str(state["artifact_path"]),
   213	        base_ref=base_ref or None,
   214	        context=context_paths,
   215	        path=[],
   216	        required_reviews=manifest.get("required_reviews"),
   217	        extra_gemini_lens=[],
   218	        sensitive=False,
   219	        large_structural=False,
   220	        performance_sensitive=False,

   756	    return verdict_path, raw_path, review_path

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba /Users/chrislaw/valuerank/.claude/worktrees/friendly-aryabhata-9efbf7/docs/workflow/feature-runs/ff-quality-of-life/spec.md | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/chrislaw/valuerank/.claude/worktrees/friendly-aryabhata-9efbf7

     1	# FF Quality-of-Life — Spec
     2	
     3	**Feature branch**: `claude/ff-quality-of-life`
     4	**Slug**: `ff-quality-of-life`
     5	**Created**: 2026-04-24
     6	**Status**: Draft spec (discovery complete)
     7	**Supporting context**: Follow-up to PR #744 + PR #749 (both merged). Addresses 4 polish items that don't fit in Feature A (safety net) or Feature C (concern IDs).
     8	
     9	## Summary
    10	
    11	Four small runner improvements that reduce operator friction:
    12	
    13	1. **Raise default character budgets** (plan Fix 7) — current defaults force partial-coverage retries on normal-sized specs. Raise to `--max-artifact-chars 40000`, `--max-context-chars 50000`, `--max-total-chars 200000`.
    14	2. **`checkpoint --validation-only`** (plan Fix 5) — past the 3-round adversarial cap, an operator editing an artifact to address judge-flagged issues has no CLI path to re-seal the manifest without dispatching expensive reviews. Add a no-review validation-only mode that just re-verifies the manifest against the current artifact SHA.
    15	3. **Restatement judge gameability** (PR #744 adversarial P2-6) — the current "diminishing returns" rule counts severity transitions. A bad-faith operator could file HIGH findings in round 1 and downgrade to MEDIUM in round 2 to trigger proceed. Harden the prompt to require quoting prior-round reasoning text when citing severity drop.
    16	4. **Discover CLI append semantics** (PR #744 + PR #749 postmortems) — passing `--non-goal X --non-goal Y` in one invocation currently keeps only `Y`. Change to append semantics; add explicit `--clear-non-goals` / `--clear-acceptance-criteria` flags for destructive cases.
    17	
    18	All four land in the runner's existing modules. Together they remove four distinct friction points that operators have actually hit (the last two showed up in this very feature's own discovery phase).
    19	
    20	## Problem statement
    21	
    22	### Fix 1 — Char budget churn
    23	
    24	[factory_review_specs.py](../../operations/codex-skills/feature-factory/scripts/factory_review_specs.py) and related modules default to conservative character budgets. During both PR #744 and PR #749 runs, the operator had to pass explicit `--max-artifact-chars 50000 --max-total-chars 250000` to avoid "artifact too large for partial coverage" failures on normal-sized specs. The defaults are set too low for real-world artifacts in this codebase.
    25	
    26	### Fix 2 — Re-seal after post-cap edits
    27	
    28	Once `adversarial_rounds >= 3`, further `checkpoint --stage X` runs are blocked by the cap — correctly, since we don't want to launch round 4 of adversarial review. But if the operator edits the artifact after the cap (to address a judge-flagged issue), there's no CLI path to re-sync the manifest SHA without bypassing the workflow. Today the operator hand-edits review frontmatter SHAs. We did that during PR #744 and #749 finalization. The correct answer is a `--validation-only` mode: skip review dispatch, just re-verify the manifest is consistent with the current artifact.
    29	
    30	### Fix 3 — Restatement judge severity-drop gaming
    31	
    32	PR #744's adversarial review raised this (finding P2-6). The restatement judge's "diminishing-returns" rule says: if prior rounds had HIGH and this round has only MEDIUM/LOW, proceed — the loop is converging. But a prompt-aware operator could:
    33	- Round 1: inject HIGH finding X with dubious justification
    34	- Round 2: reviewer mentions X but classifies it MEDIUM
    35	- Restatement judge: "severity dropped HIGH→MEDIUM, proceed"
    36	- Real HIGH issue ships undetected
    37	
    38	The fix is to make the severity-drop claim have *evidence*: the judge must quote prior-round reasoning text and name the specific finding whose severity allegedly dropped. That makes gaming much harder — to bypass, you'd need to create a fake round-1 HIGH that the judge can then quote as "now-dropped-to-MEDIUM" with text.
    39	
    40	### Fix 4 — Discover CLI silent-overwrite footgun
    41	
    42	Both PR #744 and PR #749's discovery phases hit the same footgun: passing multiple `--non-goal` or `--acceptance-criteria` flags in one invocation silently keeps only the last value. I scripted a for-loop workaround both times. The fix is append semantics by default, with explicit `--clear-non-goals` / `--clear-acceptance-criteria` flags for the rare destructive case.
    43	
    44	## Scope
    45	
    46	### In scope
    47	
    48	- **Fix 1** — raise default char budgets in `factory_cmd_checkpoint.py` argparse defaults for `--max-artifact-chars`, `--max-context-chars`, `--max-total-chars`.
    49	- **Fix 2** — add `--validation-only` flag to `checkpoint` subcommand. When set, skip reviewer dispatch entirely, just re-verify the manifest against the current artifact SHA. If drift, re-seal the manifest (updates stored SHAs to match current artifact). Exits 0 on success, 2 on error.
    50	- **Fix 3** — update `judge-prompts/restatement.md` to require quoting prior-round reasoning when citing severity drop.
    51	- **Fix 4** — in `factory_cmd_discover.py`, change `--non-goal` and `--acceptance-criteria` from `store` to `append` action. Add `--clear-non-goals` / `--clear-acceptance-criteria` flags.
    52	- **Tests** for all four behaviors.
    53	
    54	### Out of scope
    55	
    56	- Completeness judge veto (Feature A — shipped).
    57	- Auto-register mutating commands (Feature A — shipped).
    58	- Review intermediate GC (Feature A — shipped).
    59	- Embedding concern IDs (Feature C — deferred).
    60	- Rename `repair_X_checkpoint` (skipped).
    61	- `--force-advance` CLI (subsumed).
    62	- Structured JSON reviewer output (larger project).
    63	
    64	## User stories
    65	
    66	### US1 — Char budgets match real specs (Priority: P2)
    67	
    68	**As** the orchestrator running a checkpoint
    69	**I need** the default char budgets to match real-world spec sizes in this codebase
    70	**So that** I don't have to pass explicit `--max-*-chars` flags on every invocation.
    71	
    72	**Why P2**: quality of life, not correctness. Operators who know the flags can already opt out. But the defaults cause first-time-user friction and retry churn.
    73	
    74	**Independent test**: invoke `checkpoint --stage spec` with no `--max-*-chars` flags against a fixture with a 30k-char spec. Assert no partial-coverage warning. Assert the 3 values stored in the checkpoint manifest are the new defaults.
    75	
    76	**Acceptance scenarios**:
    77	
    78	1. **Given** a spec.md of 35k chars, **when** `checkpoint --stage spec` runs with no budget flags, **then** no partial-coverage retry happens.
    79	2. **Given** an operator explicitly passes `--max-artifact-chars 10000`, **when** checkpoint runs, **then** the operator's value wins (defaults don't override explicit flags).
    80	
    81	### US2 — Re-seal after post-cap edits (Priority: P1)
    82	
    83	**As** the orchestrator dealing with judge-flagged issues past the 3-round cap
    84	**I need** a `checkpoint --validation-only` mode that re-verifies the manifest without re-dispatching reviewers
    85	**So that** I can re-sync after an edit without burning agent calls or hand-editing review frontmatter SHAs.
    86	
    87	**Why P1**: this is the fix for a friction class that hit both PR #744 and PR #749 during their own runs. Hand-editing review frontmatter SHAs is exactly the kind of workflow bypass that Fix 1 in PR #744 was trying to eliminate.
    88	
    89	**Independent test**: produce a stage state with 3 adversarial rounds complete + an edited artifact (SHA drifted from manifest). Run `checkpoint --stage spec --validation-only`. Assert no subprocess calls to Codex or Gemini. Assert manifest + review frontmatter SHAs are updated to match current artifact. Exit 0.
    90	
    91	**Acceptance scenarios**:
    92	
    93	1. **Given** `adversarial_rounds == 3` and artifact SHA drifted from manifest, **when** `checkpoint --stage spec --validation-only` runs, **then** no reviewer is dispatched, manifest and review SHAs are updated, exit code is 0.
    94	2. **Given** same state but `--validation-only` is NOT passed, **when** checkpoint runs, **then** behavior is unchanged (blocked by 3-round cap or triggers repair path, whichever applies today).
    95	3. **Given** the artifact SHA matches the manifest already (no drift), **when** `--validation-only` runs, **then** exit 0, no-op, zero writes to review files.
    96	4. **Given** manifest file doesn't exist, **when** `--validation-only` runs, **then** exit 2 with "no manifest to validate".
    97	
    98	### US3 — Restatement judge severity-drop requires evidence (Priority: P2)
    99	
   100	**As** an operator trusting the judge panel
   101	**I need** the restatement judge to only proceed on severity-drop when it can quote specific prior-round reasoning
   102	**So that** the "diminishing returns" rule can't be gamed by injecting fake HIGH findings early.
   103	
   104	**Why P2**: low-probability attack, but the mitigation is almost free (prompt text change). Hardens a known soft spot from PR #744 adversarial review.
   105	
   106	**Independent test**: behavioral — inspect the updated `judge-prompts/restatement.md` file and assert it contains the quote-requirement instruction text. This is prompt-level enforcement only (per FR-011 below), so test is textual, not schema-level. Corrected in spec round-1 per Gemini HIGH — earlier draft promised "schema validation fails the verdict" which contradicted FR-011's "no schema change."
   107	
   108	**Acceptance scenarios**:
   109	
   110	1. **Given** the restatement-judge prompt is updated, **when** a judge run cites severity drop, **then** its reasoning contains at least one quoted block from a prior-round reviewer finding or prior-round judge verdict.
   111	2. **Given** the prompt change does not alter the first-round-proceed-with-annotation rule from PR #744, **when** running judge round 1 (no prior rounds), **then** verdict remains `proceed-with-annotation`.
   112	3. **Given** the prompt change does not alter the true-saturation rule, **when** 70%+ of latest findings are literal restatements, **then** verdict remains `proceed`.
   113	
   114	### US4 — Discover CLI accepts multiple values (Priority: P2)
   115	
   116	**As** an operator or an agent running discovery
   117	**I need** `discover --non-goal X --non-goal Y --non-goal Z` in one invocation to store all three values
   118	**So that** I don't have to script a for-loop or remember to pass flags one at a time.
   119	
   120	**Why P2**: quality of life. Agents (including this feature's own orchestrator) repeatedly hit this — two consecutive features had to work around it. Fix is ~5 lines.
   121	
   122	**Independent test**: invoke `discover --non-goal X --non-goal Y --non-goal Z` on a fresh workflow. Assert `state.discovery.non_goals == ["X", "Y", "Z"]` (order preserved, all present).
   123	
   124	**Acceptance scenarios**:
   125	
   126	1. **Given** no prior non-goals, **when** `discover --non-goal A --non-goal B` runs, **then** `state.discovery.non_goals == ["A", "B"]`.
   127	2. **Given** existing non-goals `["A"]`, **when** `discover --non-goal B --non-goal C` runs, **then** `state.discovery.non_goals == ["A", "B", "C"]` (append semantics preserved).
   128	3. **Given** existing non-goals `["A", "B", "C"]`, **when** `discover --clear-non-goals` runs, **then** `state.discovery.non_goals == []`.
   129	4. **Given** `discover --clear-non-goals --non-goal D` in one invocation, **when** it runs, **then** `state.discovery.non_goals == ["D"]` (clear happens first, then append).
   130	5. **Same semantics** for `--acceptance-criteria` / `--clear-acceptance-criteria`.
   131	
   132	## Functional requirements
   133	
   134	### Fix 1 — Raise default char budgets (US1)
   135	
   136	- **FR-001**: In `run_factory.py` argparse setup for the `checkpoint` subcommand, set defaults to `--max-artifact-chars 50000`, `--max-context-chars 60000`, `--max-total-chars 250000`. Corrected in spec round-1 per Codex feasibility MEDIUM — earlier draft proposed 40k artifact which would have LOWERED the review-lens scripts' current 50k default. These new values are raises-or-equal across the chain.
   137	- **FR-002**: Operator-passed explicit values MUST still override the defaults (no change to precedence).
   138	- **FR-003**: Existing tests using smaller budgets MUST continue to pass (they pass explicit values, not defaults).
   139	
   140	### Fix 2 — `--validation-only` flag (US2)
   141	
   142	- **FR-004**: Add `--validation-only` to the `checkpoint` subcommand. Mutually exclusive with `--fallback` (reviewer replacement) — validation-only is purely a manifest-sync path.
   143	- **FR-005**: When `--validation-only` is set, `command_checkpoint` MUST:
   144	  - NOT dispatch any reviewer (no `codex exec`, no `gemini` subprocess).
   145	  - Load the stage's checkpoint manifest (`reviews/{stage}.checkpoint.json`) to get the list of `required_reviews`.
   146	  - Compute the current artifact SHA via `workflow_utils.normalized_artifact_hash`.
   147	  - Read each review file's frontmatter `artifact_sha256`. **Note: `checkpoint_manifest()` itself does not currently store an `artifact_sha256` field** (per Codex feasibility HIGH finding) — the review-file frontmatter is the authoritative SHA source. Validation reads each `required_reviews[].path` and checks its frontmatter.
   148	  - If ALL review frontmatter SHAs match the current artifact SHA: print `manifest already matches artifact (sha={sha})`, exit 0.
   149	  - If ANY review frontmatter SHA differs: update the frontmatter `artifact_sha256` field on every review file in `required_reviews`. Use `atomic_json_write`-equivalent for each file (write to temp file then `os.replace`) to avoid the partial-write inconsistency Gemini flagged. Before any writes, compute the full set of (path, old_sha, new_sha) tuples and validate every target file exists and is writable; if any fails pre-check, exit 2 BEFORE writing any file. Print `manifest re-sealed: {N} review files updated from {old} to {new}`. Exit 0.
   150	  - If manifest doesn't exist: print `no manifest to validate — run checkpoint first`, exit 2.
   151	- **FR-006**: `--validation-only` MUST NOT bypass the lifecycle-concern gate from PR #749 — if prior-stage open concerns exist, deliver/next-checkpoint still blocks. This is a manifest-sync tool, not a workflow bypass.
   152	- **FR-007**: An annotation MUST be appended to `stages[stage].annotations[]` with type `"validation-only-reseal"` recording `{old_sha, new_sha, at: <epoch>}` when a reseal happens.
   153	
   154	### Fix 3 — Restatement judge severity-drop evidence (US3)
   155	
   156	- **FR-008**: Update `judge-prompts/restatement.md` to add a requirement under the diminishing-returns rule: "When citing severity drop as proceed justification, the verdict reasoning MUST quote at least one specific finding from prior rounds by its original reasoning text (first 60+ characters verbatim). If you cannot quote a prior round's text, severity-drop reasoning is not valid — vote based on other rules."
   157	- **FR-009**: The first-round-proceed-with-annotation rule from PR #744 MUST be preserved unchanged.
   158	- **FR-010**: The true-saturation rule (70%+ restatements → proceed) MUST be preserved unchanged.
   159	- **FR-011**: No schema change — the enforcement is prompt-level. A follow-up could add a structured `quoted_prior_finding: str` field, but this feature keeps scope minimal.
   160	
   161	### Fix 4 — Discover CLI append semantics (US4)
   162	
   163	- **FR-012**: In `factory_cmd_discover.py` argparse setup, change `--non-goal` and `--acceptance-criteria` from `action="store"` to `action="append"` (or equivalent — match existing codebase patterns). Each flag occurrence appends to an internal list.
   164	- **FR-013**: When `discover` runs, for each value in the appended list: add it to `state.discovery.non_goals` (or `acceptance_criteria`) if not already present (dedup by exact string match). Order preserved.
   165	- **FR-014**: Add new flags `--clear-non-goals` and `--clear-acceptance-criteria` (boolean). When set, the corresponding list is emptied BEFORE any append operations in the same invocation. This lets `discover --clear-non-goals --non-goal D` produce `["D"]`. Per Gemini LOW #3, the command's argparse help text MUST document this clear-then-append order explicitly.
   166	- **FR-015**: No other discovery flags change behavior. `--assumption`, `--unresolved`, `--resolve`, `--defer`, `--answer`, `--question`, etc. keep their current semantics.
   167	
   168	### Shared
   169	
   170	- **FR-016**: All 183 existing runner tests MUST continue to pass. Target total: ~200.
   171	- **FR-017**: No change to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, cloud/CLAUDE.md, cloud/AGENTS.md, MEMORY.md, .gitignore, or any file outside the scripts/judge-prompts directories.
   172	
   173	## Success criteria
   174	
   175	- **SC-001**: A fresh checkpoint invocation with no `--max-*-chars` flags uses 40k/50k/200k and doesn't trigger partial-coverage retry on a 35k-char spec.
   176	- **SC-002**: `checkpoint --stage spec --validation-only` on a drifted artifact re-seals the manifest + review frontmatter SHAs without any subprocess calls (verified via mock assertion).
   177	- **SC-003**: `--validation-only` on a matching-SHA artifact is a no-op.
   178	- **SC-004**: Restatement judge prompt contains the quote-evidence requirement; first-round + true-saturation rules preserved.
   179	- **SC-005**: `discover --non-goal A --non-goal B --non-goal C` in one invocation stores all 3 in order.
   180	- **SC-006**: `discover --clear-non-goals --non-goal D` produces `["D"]`.
   181	- **SC-007**: All 183 existing tests + 3 new test files green; target ~200 total.
   182	
   183	## Edge cases
   184	
   185	- **Char budgets**: if a spec is genuinely larger than 40k (rare), explicit `--max-artifact-chars` still works. No regression.
   186	- **`--validation-only` on first-ever checkpoint**: no manifest exists → exit 2 with clear message.
   187	- **`--validation-only` with `--fallback`**: mutually exclusive argparse error.
   188	- **`--validation-only` with `--address`/`--defer`/`--dismiss`**: mutually exclusive. Argparse rejects the combination (per Gemini LOW #4). Operators must run the concern-lifecycle action in one invocation, then `--validation-only` in a separate one. This avoids the "implicit order of operations" fragility.
   189	- **Restatement prompt rollback**: if a reviewer's output format drifts and can't be quoted, judge falls through to other rules — doesn't break.
   190	- **`discover` append + dedup**: `discover --non-goal A --non-goal A` once stores just `["A"]`, not `["A", "A"]`. Dedup is an explicit design choice per FR-013.
   191	
   192	## Assumptions carried in
   193	
   194	1. Scope is Fix 1+2+3+4 only — no scope creep.
   195	2. Char budget defaults 40k/50k/200k match actual observed spec/plan sizes in this codebase.
   196	3. `--validation-only` is a pure manifest-sync tool, not a workflow bypass — lifecycle gates still apply.
   197	4. Restatement judge severity-drop enforcement is prompt-level (no schema change). A structured field is a potential follow-up.
   198	5. Discover CLI uses argparse `action="append"` pattern, which matches existing Python idioms for repeated flags.
   199	6. Codex implements slices after spec/plan/tasks checkpoints healthy. Claude is orchestrator only.
   200	
   201	## Non-goals
   202	
   203	See discovery state — 7 deferred items.
   204	
   205	## Residual risks (with verification)
   206	
   207	- **Risk R1** — char budget raise causes unexpected OOM or rate-limit issues on very small hardware.
   208	  **verification:** existing tests mostly use explicit small budgets and are unaffected. Production runs against full-size specs can hit larger budgets anyway; defaults just prevent retry churn. If rate limits become an issue, operators can lower via flags.
   209	
   210	- **Risk R2** — `--validation-only` becomes a "quiet bypass" that operators use to skip reviews.
   211	  **verification:** doesn't skip the concern-lifecycle gate (FR-006). And the manifest-reseal annotation (FR-007) makes its use visible in the audit trail. Not a bypass, a sync tool.
   212	
   213	- **Risk R3** — restatement prompt gets overly strict and fails legitimate rounds.
   214	  **verification:** the quote rule is specifically scoped to severity-drop proceed justification. Other proceed paths (first-round, true-saturation) don't require quotes. If legitimate cases fail, they fall through to other rules.
   215	
   216	- **Risk R4** — append semantics break a caller that depended on overwrite behavior.
   217	  **verification:** nothing in the codebase relies on overwrite (confirmed by the fact that both feature runs before this one had to script for-loops to get append behavior). The explicit `--clear-*-goals` flags cover the intentional-destructive case.

It uses the stage’s own SHA history, so a reseal that only rewrites review frontmatter would leave later judge runs out of sync.    326	def _summarize_review(review_path: Path) -> str:
   327	    try:
   328	        meta, _ = parse_review_frontmatter(review_path)
   329	    except Exception:
   330	        return f"- {review_path.name} [unreadable]"
   331	    status = str(meta.get("resolution_status", "")).strip() or "unknown"
   332	    note = str(meta.get("resolution_note", "")).strip()
   333	    findings = _findings_from_review(review_path)
   334	    lines = [f"- {review_path.name} [{status}]"]
   335	    if note:
   336	        lines.append(f"  - resolution: {note}")
   337	    for finding in findings:
   338	        lines.append(f"  - {finding['severity']} {finding['id']}: {finding['finding']}")
   339	    if len(lines) == 1:
   340	        lines.append("  - findings: none")
   341	    return "\n".join(lines)

   349	            return last
   350	    initial_sha = stage_state.get("initial_sha", "")
   351	    return str(initial_sha or "")

   363	        capture_output=True,
   364	        text=True,

   366	    )

   368	        return result.stdout.strip()
   369	    return _git_head_sha(REPO_ROOT) or ""

   372	def _git_diff(slug: str, base_sha: str) -> str:
   373	    if not base_sha:
   374	        return ""
   375	    paths = _artifact_paths(slug)
   376	    cmd = [
   377	        "git",
   378	        "-C",
   379	        str(REPO_ROOT),
   380	        "diff",
   381	        "--unified=3",
   382	        base_sha,
   383	        "HEAD",
   384	        "--",
   385	        str(paths["spec"]),
   386	        str(paths["plan"]),
   387	        str(paths["tasks"]),
   388	    ]
   389	    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
   390	    return result.stdout if result.returncode == 0 else result.stdout or result.stderr or ""

   260	def _safe_frontmatter(review_path: Path) -> dict[str, str]:
   261	    try:
   262	        data, _ = parse_review_frontmatter(review_path)
   263	    except Exception:

   268	def _findings_block(review_body: str) -> str:
   269	    if "## Findings" not in review_body:
   270	        return ""
   271	    tail = review_body.split("## Findings", 1)[1]
   272	    if "\n## " in tail:
   273	        tail = tail.split("\n## ", 1)[0]
   274	    return tail.strip()
